body: This showed up when running scripts by @sandreza and @xiaozhour .

labels.description: Even a perfect program still has bugs
labels.name: bug 🐞

title: Fix verbose printing of file writing time in JLD2OutputWriter

body:
This introduces `UpwindBiasedFirstOrder` advection, and is in part inspired by #1955.

This scheme is first order accurate but has the advantage of preserving positivity and extrema.

The plot for convergence is updated and we see that it does have the slope that is predicted from the theory.

![convergence_rates](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8239041/130524906-d3afbae0-651d-4c9a-aee8-b0fb4d9dc856.png)

I set the `symmetric_interpolate`'s to zero, which I think is true, but not sure if anything else should be done here.
title: introduces `UpwindBiasedFirstOrder` advection

body: I feel this is small positive change that's a bit more specific and informative. I don't think we need to advertise the dimensionality (runs in 1-3 dimensions...); it's a perk that 2D and 1D simulations are easy, but not a crucial code feature to be advertised on this first line.
title: Small change to README description
